name: Create LinkedIn carousel post from blog post

parameters.content:
## Create LinkedIn carousel post from blog post

### How it works

1. A manual trigger reads the newest article from your blog's RSS feed.
2. An LLM turns the article into slide copy, which is parsed and split into individual slides.
3. For each slide a background image is generated with an AI model, downloaded, and uploaded to Posta.
4. The slides are re-assembled and rendered into a text carousel PDF.
5. Posta publishes the carousel to your connected LinkedIn account.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Add your Posta credential (API token) to the Posta nodes
- [ ] Add your DeepSeek (LLM) credential to the 'Basic LLM Chain' model
- [ ] Add your fal.ai credential for AI background generation
- [ ] Set the RSS feed URL and pick the LinkedIn account in 'Get many social accounts'

### Customization

Adjust the number of slides and the 'theme_color' to match your brand styling.

## Fetch latest blog post

Starts the run, reads the RSS feed, and selects the latest post.

## Load target accounts

Fetches the connected LinkedIn account to publish to.

## Write and split slides

An LLM writes the carousel copy, which is parsed and split into individual slides.

## Generate slide backgrounds

Sets the theme color and generates, downloads, and uploads an AI background per slide.

## Assemble and publish carousel

Re-assembles the slides into a text carousel PDF and posts it to LinkedIn.

nodes.name: RSS Read
nodes.notes: Add the link to the RSS feed

parameters.text:
=Turn the article below into a 5-slide LinkedIn carousel.

  Rules:
  - Exactly 5 slides.
  - Slide 1 = a scroll-stopping hook. Slides 2–4 = the key takeaways (one idea per slide). Slide 5 = a clear call to action.
  - Each slide has a short "title" (max ~6 words) and a "body" of 2–3 punchy lines (max ~360 characters). Plain text, no markdown, no emojis in the title.
   Each slide also has an "image_prompt": a VERY short phrase describing one or two soft simple shapes and 2 muted colors on a deep background — e.g. "a soft coral orb on
  deep plum" or "two gentle mauve shapes on deep indigo". Keep colors muted and gentle, never neon or harsh. Shapes and colors only — no scenes, no nature, no people, no text.
  - Also write 3–5 relevant "tags" (hashtag words, WITHOUT the # symbol) and a 1–2 sentence LinkedIn "caption" to accompany the carousel.
  - Base everything strictly on the article — do not invent facts, names, or numbers.

  Return ONLY raw minified JSON — no markdown, no code fences, no commentary:
  {"caption":"...","tags":["...","..."],"slides":[{"title":"...","body":"...","image_prompt":"..."}]}

  ARTICLE TITLE: {{ $json.title }}

  ARTICLE:
  {{ $json["content:encoded"] }}

messageValues.message: You are an expert LinkedIn carousel copywriter. You always return valid minified JSON and nothing else.

parameters.value:
={{ $json.theme_color }} smooth gradient, minimal flat graphic background, a couple of subtle soft tonal shapes in the same color slightly lighter, clean modern,
  lots of plain empty space, professional, no text

parameters.slides: ={{ JSON.stringify($json.slides.map(s => ({ media_id: s.media_id, title: s.title, body: s.body }))) }}
